skill.name: azure-security-keyvault-keys-java
skill.description: Azure Key Vault Keys Java SDK for cryptographic key management. Use when creating, managing, or using RSA/EC keys, performing encrypt/decrypt/sign/verify operations, or working with HSM-backed keys.
skill.summary: Azure Key Vault Keys Java SDK for cryptographic key management in Azure cloud environments.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This skill is Azure Key Vault Keys Java SDK documentation. Static analysis flagged 84 patterns (backticks in markdown code blocks, URL placeholders, and API references) that are all false positives. The file contains legitimate cryptographic API examples with no executable code, no command injection vectors, and no credential exfiltration patterns. Safe for publication.

low_findings.title: Deprecated Algorithm Documentation
low_findings.description: Documentation mentions RSA1_5 algorithm (line 309) which is cryptographically weak. However, the documentation correctly marks RSA_OAEP as recommended and this is informational only, not actual implementation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: RSA1_5 is documented as an available algorithm option but the skill correctly recommends RSA_OAEP. This is documentation transparency, not a security vulnerability.

content.user_title: Manage Azure Key Vault cryptographic keys with Java SDK
content.value_statement: Securely manage cryptographic keys and perform encryption operations in Azure Key Vault. This skill provides complete Java SDK documentation for RSA, EC, and HSM-backed key management.
actual_capabilities: Create and manage RSA, EC, and symmetric keys in Azure Key Vault and Managed HSM
Perform cryptographic operations: encrypt, decrypt, sign, verify, wrap, and unwrap keys
Configure key rotation policies and automatic expiration
Backup and restore keys with portable key material
Manage HSM-protected keys with hardware security modules
Handle key versions and soft-delete recovery
limitations: Requires Azure subscription and Key Vault instance with proper access policies configured
Network connectivity to Azure Key Vault endpoint required for all operations
HSM-backed keys require Managed HSM or premium Key Vault tier
Symmetric key operations (OCT) only supported in Managed HSM, not standard Key Vault

use_cases.title: Enterprise Key Management
use_cases.description: Manage encryption keys for enterprise applications storing sensitive data. Create HSM-backed RSA keys for maximum security, configure automatic rotation policies, and maintain audit trails for compliance requirements.

use_cases.title: Application Data Encryption
use_cases.description: Implement envelope encryption patterns using Key Vault keys. Wrap symmetric data keys with RSA keys stored in Key Vault, perform encrypt/decrypt operations, and manage key versions for seamless rotation.

use_cases.title: Digital Signature Operations
use_cases.description: Sign and verify digital signatures using EC or RSA keys in Key Vault. Generate SHA-256 digests, sign with HSM-protected keys, and verify signatures for document integrity and authentication workflows.

prompt_templates.title: Create an RSA Key
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 2048-bit RSA key in Azure Key Vault named 'production-key' with encryption and decryption operations enabled.

prompt_templates.title: Encrypt and Decrypt Data
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to encrypt a string using RSA_OAEP algorithm with a Key Vault key, then decrypt the ciphertext back to plaintext.

prompt_templates.title: Configure Key Rotation Policy
prompt_templates.prompt: Set up automatic key rotation for 'my-key' to rotate every 90 days, with the rotation action triggered 30 days before expiration.

prompt_templates.title: Implement Envelope Encryption
prompt_templates.prompt: Demonstrate envelope encryption: generate a 256-bit AES key locally, wrap it using an RSA key from Key Vault, then unwrap and use the AES key for local encryption.

output_examples.input: Create a 4096-bit RSA key with expiration set to one year from now
output_examples.output: Key successfully created with name 'my-rsa-key-4096', ID 'https://vault-name.vault.azure.net/keys/my-rsa-key-4096', enabled status true, and expiration date set to 2027-02-25.

output_examples.input: Encrypt 'Hello, World!' using RSA_OAEP and the key 'encryption-key'
output_examples.output: Plaintext encrypted successfully. Ciphertext length: 256 bytes. Decryption verified - original message recovered: 'Hello, World!'

output_examples.input: Sign data and verify the signature using RS256 algorithm
output_examples.output: Data digest computed using SHA-256 (32 bytes). Signature generated: 256 bytes. Verification result: Valid signature confirmed.

best_practices: Use HSM-protected keys (setHardwareProtected=true) for production workloads handling sensitive data
Enable soft-delete on Key Vault to protect against accidental key deletion and allow recovery
Implement key rotation policies with automatic rotation before expiration to maintain security compliance
anti_patterns: Using RSA1_5 algorithm for encryption - this algorithm is deprecated and vulnerable to padding oracle attacks. Always use RSA_OAEP or RSA_OAEP_256.
Storing key backup files without encryption - backup key material is sensitive and should be encrypted at rest with access controls.
Granting excessive key permissions - follow least privilege principle by limiting key operations to only what the application requires.

faq.question: What is the difference between standard Key Vault and Managed HSM?
faq.answer: Managed HSM provides single-tenant, FIPS 140-2 Level 3 validated HSMs with support for HSM-protected keys (RSA_HSM, EC_HSM, OCT_HSM). Standard Key Vault supports software-protected keys and some HSM operations but lacks single-tenant isolation.

faq.question: How does key rotation work in Azure Key Vault?
faq.answer: Key rotation creates a new version of the key while preserving the key name. You can manually call rotateKey() or configure automatic rotation policies with KeyRotationPolicy that trigger rotation at specified intervals before expiration.

faq.question: Can I import my own key material into Key Vault?
faq.answer: Yes, use the importKey() method with a JsonWebKey containing your key components (modulus, exponent, private exponent for RSA). Set hardwareProtected=true to import into HSM if using Managed HSM.

faq.question: What happens when I delete a key from Key Vault?
faq.answer: With soft-delete enabled (default), deletion is reversible. The key enters a deleted state for the retention period (7-90 days). Use recoverDeletedKey() to restore, or purgeDeletedKey() for permanent deletion.

faq.question: How do I authenticate to Azure Key Vault?
faq.answer: Use DefaultAzureCredential which tries multiple authentication methods: managed identity, Azure CLI, Visual Studio credentials, environment variables, and Azure PowerShell. Configure AZURE_KEYVAULT_URL environment variable with your vault endpoint.

faq.question: What cryptographic algorithms are supported?
faq.answer: Encryption: RSA_OAEP, RSA_OAEP_256, A128GCM, A256GCM. Signatures: RS256, RS384, RS512, PS256, ES256, ES384, ES512. Key wrap: RSA_OAEP. Avoid deprecated algorithms like RSA1_5 for new applications.
